branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The K & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Royals pride

columnists.name: Bobby Lee Chamberlain
columnists.backstory: A Kansas City, Kansas reporter who covered the 2015 World Series and still quotes Ned Yost at every available opportunity.
columnists.voice_sample: Witt Jr. singled home two, Lugo pitched into the seventh, and Kansas City beat the Twins 4-2 at Kauffman Stadium.

columnists.name: Dottie Verlander (no relation)
columnists.backstory: An Overland Park BBQ caterer who has been telling everyone Bobby Witt Jr. is the next George Brett since he was in Double-A.
columnists.voice_sample: A three-game skid? Please. Witt's cleats haven't even warmed up. This lineup is a slow-burn brisket and by June you'll be begging for seconds.

columnists.name: Cletus Marburger
columnists.backstory: A North Kansas City retiree who was at Game 6 in 1985 and has been waiting for the next one ever since with deepening skepticism.
columnists.voice_sample: You get one or two good players, they get traded. You get a ring, they fire-sale the roster. David Glass lives in every Royals decision, even now.
